entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop (Sorghum bicolor) affected by ergot disease

entities.name: ERGOT
entities.description: A fungus that causes ergot disease in sorghum

entities.name: ERGOT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to resist infection by ergot

entities.name: FLOWERING BIOLOGY
entities.description: The biological processes related to the flowering of the plant

entities.name: ERGOT INFECTION
entities.description: The state of being infected by the ergot fungus

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL FIELD-SCREENING TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A method developed to identify ergot resistance in sorghum

entities.name: ANTHESIS
entities.description: The period during which a flower is fully open and functional

entities.name: TRIMMING OF PANICLES
entities.description: The process of removing pollinated spikelets before inoculation

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The act of introducing the ergot fungus into the plant

entities.name: PANICLE BAGGING
entities.description: The process of covering panicles for 7-10 days after inoculation

entities.name: QUALITATIVE VISUAL RATING METHOD
entities.description: A 1-5 scale method used to evaluate ergot infection

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE SPIKELET COUNTING METHOD
entities.description: A method of counting spikelets to evaluate ergot infection

entities.name: KARAMA RESEARCH STATION, RWANDA
entities.description: A research facility in Rwanda where sorghum was screened

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTRE, INDIA
entities.description: A research facility in India where sorghum was screened

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where ergot is an important disease of sorghum

entities.description: A continent where ergot is an important disease of sorghum
